title: fraxinus pubescens var. nana
intro: Fraxinus pubescens var. nana, also known as the dwarf Oregon ash or small Oregon ash, is a small deciduous shrub or tree that belongs to the Oleaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which help to soothe and protect wounds. The health benefits of Fraxinus pubescens var. nana include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
